name: Western Union
description_short: Western Union at 101 S Tryon St Ste 22, Charlotte, NC 28280 in the Charlotte center city neighborhood is open 8:00 AM – 9:00 PM daily. Call +1 704-334-6262.
description_long: Western Union has established itself as a leading provider of money transfer and financial services since its founding, operating one of the world's largest networks of physical agent locations. The company serves customers seeking to send money domestically and internationally, with the ability to pick up cash at thousands of agent locations including retail partners like Walmart, Safeway, City Market, and Kroger. Their service portfolio encompasses wire transfers, bill payments, mobile top-ups, money orders, prepaid card services, and inmate payment options, accessible both online and in-store. Western Union distinguishes itself through its massive distribution network—demonstrated by the location finder showing hundreds of agent locations in a single state—and 24/7 online functionality paired with in-store pickup convenience during agent hours. The company's strength lies in accessibility and established trust for international remittances, particularly among immigrant communities sending money to family abroad. However, Western Union's fees are typically higher than digital alternatives, and the service remains dependent on physical agent locations for cash pickup, limiting convenience in rural areas.
address: 101 S Tryon St Ste 22, Charlotte, NC 28280

company_info.headquarters: 101 S Tryon St Ste 22, Charlotte, NC 28280

pros: Extensive physical agent network with 500,000+ locations globally, including major retailers like Walmart, Safeway, and City Market
24/7 online money sending capability paired with flexible in-store payment options
Multiple payment methods including online initiation with in-store pickup
International money transfer capability with multi-language support (English, Spanish, Haitian Creole, Chinese, French, Vietnamese)
Diverse service offerings including bill payments, mobile top-ups, money orders, and inmate payments
Real-time transfer tracking functionality available online
Western Union Rewards program with referral incentives
cons: Transaction fees are significantly higher than digital money transfer alternatives like Venmo, PayPal, or newer remittance apps
Dependent on physical agent location hours for cash pickup, creating inconvenience for users needing after-hours access
Website navigation issues evident from 404 errors on secondary pages, suggesting potential user experience problems
No mention of competitive exchange rates for international transfers compared to specialty remittance providers
Limited transparency on their website regarding specific fee structures and exchange rate details
best_for: Immigrants and individuals sending money internationally to family in countries with limited digital banking infrastructure
Unbanked and underbanked consumers without access to traditional bank accounts who need cash pickup services
Customers preferring established, trusted brands for large international transfers despite higher fees
Individuals needing to pay bills or send money to incarcerated individuals through specialized payment services
diagnosis: Western Union is best for unbanked consumers and international remittance senders who prioritize established brand trust and extensive physical access over cost efficiency. The main caveat is that fees are substantially higher than modern digital alternatives, making it primarily suitable for customers in corridors where recipients lack digital banking access or who specifically need cash pickup rather than account deposit.
services: Domestic and international money wire transfers
